title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard that allows websites to expose structured, named, typed tools to AI agents instead of requiring them to parse the DOM and perform pixel-level interactions. It offers both declarative HTML attributes and imperative JavaScript APIs for defining agent-callable actions, and is currently in early preview on Chrome 146.
writeup: This talk introduces WebMCP, a proposed web standard that lets websites expose structured, named, typed tools to AI agents rather than forcing agents to parse the DOM and simulate pixel-level interactions. It covers two ways to define agent-callable actions: declarative HTML attributes and imperative JavaScript APIs. The shift matters because it reframes agent interaction as calling published actions instead of scraping and clicking through interfaces. WebMCP is in early preview on Chrome 146.
